title: 16" Deep Bookshelf Base
summary: A deep, fixed-shelf plywood base module — one part of a mix-and-match desk or storage system, meant to sit under a shared desktop.
description:
This is a component build rather than a finished piece on its own: a simple two-shelf plywood box, sized 16" deep so it reads as a proper bookshelf rather than a shallow desk pedestal, meant to stand alongside other base modules under a shared desktop. Fixed shelves rather than adjustable ones keep the box itself rigid, which matters more here than in a standalone bookcase since this base also has to carry the desktop's load without racking.

The back panel is optional in the source but worth adding even though it isn't strictly needed to hold books: it's attached to the center shelf specifically for extra support, the same logic behind adding backing to any wide, deep shelf carrying real weight. If you're building more than one base for a full system, cut every module's sides from the same sheet in one session — sheet goods vary slightly between batches, and matching modules built weeks apart is where an otherwise-invisible seam shows up.

materials.name: Plywood, 3/4", half sheet
materials.note: Ripped into 15-3/4" wide strips at the store — about 12 ft total needed.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two sides at 29-1/4" and two shelves at 39-1/2" from the Plywood. Cut two bottom footer at 39-1/2" from the Pine. Cut two top supports at 39-1/2" from the Pine. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to one 8 ft Pine and one 8 ft Pine. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Attach the footer
steps.body: Attach the 1x4 footer boards to the bottom of the sides with pocket holes, all set on the 3/4" jig setting with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach the shelves
steps.body: Attach the shelves with pocket holes as fixed shelves — fixed rather than adjustable is what keeps this base rigid enough to carry a desktop's load. Also attach the middle shelf to the footer boards with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue for extra reinforcement.

steps.title: Attach the top supports and desktop
steps.body:
Attach the 1x2 top supports with pocket holes on top — the desktop will hide them once installed. Attach the desktop (minimum 16" wide x 41" long) from underneath into these 1x2s.

A back panel from 1/4" plywood, attached to the center shelf, adds support if this base will carry real weight regularly.

images.alt: 16-inch deep bookshelf base for a desk or storage system
